title: Layout
description: The #[Layout] attribute specifies which Blade layout a full-page component should use, allowing you to customize layouts on a per-component basis.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
Apply the #[Layout] attribute to a full-page component to use a specific layout:
This component will render using the resources/views/layouts/dashboard.blade.php layout instead of the default.

examples.code:
Posts

@foreach ($this->posts as $post)
{{ $post->title }}
@endforeach

sections.title: #Default layout
sections.content:
By default, Livewire uses the layout specified in your config/livewire.php file:
The #[Layout] attribute overrides this default for specific components.

sections.title: #Passing data to layouts
sections.content:
You can pass additional data to your layout using array syntax:
In your layout file, the $title variable will be available:

sections.title: #Alternative: Using layout() method
sections.content:
Instead of the attribute, you can use the layout() method in your render() method:
The attribute approach is cleaner for single-file components that don't need a render() method.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/pages/posts/⚡index.blade.php use Livewire\Component; new class extends Component { public function render() { return view('livewire.posts.index') ->layout('layouts::dashboard', ['title' => 'Posts']); }};

sections.title: #Using different layouts per page
sections.content: A common pattern is to use different layouts for different sections of your app:

examples.code: // Admin pagesnew #[Layout('layouts::admin')] class extends Component { } // Marketing pagesnew #[Layout('layouts::marketing')] class extends Component { } // Dashboard pagesnew #[Layout('layouts::dashboard')] class extends Component { }

sections.title: #When to use
sections.content:
Use #[Layout] when:
The #[Layout] attribute only applies to full-page components. Regular components that are rendered within other views don't use layouts.

sections.title: #Learn more
sections.content: For more information about full-page components and layouts, see the Pages documentation.
